Apache NuttX is a POSIX-compliant real-time operating system for microcontrollers. It has arch-specific startup code, board support packages, character/block device drivers, filesystems, networking (TCP/IP, CAN, I2C, SPI), and scheduling. Pure C codebase.

There is a GPIO pin multiplexing conflict where same pin is configured for two different functions downstream of function host_rename in the arch/sim/src subsystem.
The function is located at arch/sim/src/sim/posix/sim_hostfs.c:595.
Please investigate and fix this issue.

   102	/****************************************************************************
   103	 * Name: sim_gpiochip_direction
   104	 *
   105	 * Description:
   106	 *   Provide gpiochip pin direction config.
   107	 *
   108	 * Input Parameters:
   109	 *   dev       - A pointer to instance of sim gpiochip device.
   110	 *   pin       - The pin number.
   111	 *   direction - The direction of the pin.
   112	 *
   113	 * Returned Value:
   114	 *   0 for success, other for fail.
   115	 ****************************************************************************/
   116	
   117	static int sim_gpiochip_direction(struct ioexpander_dev_s *dev,
   118	                                  uint8_t pin, int direction)
   119	{
   120	  struct sim_gpiochip_dev_s *priv = (struct sim_gpiochip_dev_s *)dev;
   121	
   122	  if (direction == IOEXPANDER_DIRECTION_OUT ||
   123	      direction == IOEXPANDER_DIRECTION_OUT_OPENDRAIN)
   124	    {
   125	      return host_gpiochip_direction(priv->dev, pin, false);
   126	    }
   127	  else
   128	    {
   129	      return host_gpiochip_direction(priv->dev, pin, true);
   130	    }
   131	}
   132	
   133	/****************************************************************************
   134	 * Name: sim_gpiochip_option
   135	 *
   136	 * Description:
   137	 *   Provide gpiochip pin option.
   138	 *
   139	 * Input Parameters:
   140	 *   dev    - A pointer to instance of sim gpiochip device.
   141	 *   pin    - The pin number.
   142	 *   option.- The option type.
   143	 *   val    - A pointer to val of the pin.
   144	 *
   145	 * Returned Value:
   146	 *   0 for success, other for fail.
   147	 ****************************************************************************/
   148	
   149	static int sim_gpiochip_option(struct ioexpander_dev_s *dev, uint8_t pin,
   150	                               int option, void *val)
   151	{
   152	  struct sim_gpiochip_dev_s *priv = (struct sim_gpiochip_dev_s *)dev;
   153	  uint16_t cfgset = 0;
   154	  int ret = 0;
   155	
   156	  if (option == IOEXPANDER_OPTION_INTCFG)
   157	    {
   158	      switch ((uintptr_t)val)
   159	        {
   160	          case IOEXPANDER_VAL_FALLING:
   161	            cfgset = GPIOCHIP_LINE_FLAG_FALLING;
   162	            break;
   163	
   164	          case IOEXPANDER_VAL_RISING:
   165	            cfgset = GPIOCHIP_LINE_FLAG_RISING;
   166	            break;
   167	
   168	          case IOEXPANDER_VAL_BOTH:
   169	            cfgset = GPIOCHIP_LINE_FLAG_BOTH;
   170	            break;
   171	
   172	          case IOEXPANDER_VAL_DISABLE:
   173	            cfgset = GPIOCHIP_LINE_FLAG_DISABLE;
   174	            break;
   175	
   176	          default:
   177	            return -ENOTSUP;
   178	        }
   179	
   180	      ret = host_gpiochip_irq_request(priv->dev, pin, cfgset);
   181	      if (ret < 0)
   182	        {
   183	          gpioerr("ERROR: Failed to request event: %s\n", strerror(errno));
   184	        }
   185	    }
   186	  else
   187	    {
   188	      gpioinfo("gpiochip io option not support\n");
   189	      return -ENOTSUP;
   190	    }
   191	
   192	  return ret;
   193	}

    50	
    51	int sim_gpio_initialize(void)
    52	{
    53	  /* Get an instance of the simulated I/O expander */
    54	
    55	  struct ioexpander_dev_s *ioe = ioe_dummy_initialize();
    56	  if (ioe == NULL)
    57	    {
    58	      gpioerr("ERROR: ioe_dummy_initialize failed\n");
    59	      return -ENOMEM;
    60	    }
    61	
    62	  /* Register four pin drivers */
    63	
    64	  /* Pin 0: an non-inverted, input pin */
    65	
    66	  IOEXP_SETDIRECTION(ioe, 0, IOEXPANDER_DIRECTION_IN);
    67	  IOEXP_SETOPTION(ioe, 0, IOEXPANDER_OPTION_INVERT,
    68	                  (void *)IOEXPANDER_VAL_NORMAL);
    69	  IOEXP_SETOPTION(ioe, 0, IOEXPANDER_OPTION_INTCFG,
    70	                  (void *)IOEXPANDER_VAL_DISABLE);
    71	  gpio_lower_half(ioe, 0, GPIO_INPUT_PIN, 0);
    72	
    73	  /* Pin 1: an non-inverted, output pin */
    74	
    75	  IOEXP_SETDIRECTION(ioe, 1, IOEXPANDER_DIRECTION_OUT);
    76	  IOEXP_SETOPTION(ioe, 1, IOEXPANDER_OPTION_INVERT,
    77	                  (void *)IOEXPANDER_VAL_NORMAL);
    78	  IOEXP_SETOPTION(ioe, 1, IOEXPANDER_OPTION_INTCFG,
    79	                  (void *)IOEXPANDER_VAL_DISABLE);
    80	  gpio_lower_half(ioe, 1, GPIO_OUTPUT_PIN, 1);
    81	
    82	  /* Pin 2: an non-inverted, edge interrupting pin */
    83	
    84	  IOEXP_SETDIRECTION(ioe, 2, IOEXPANDER_DIRECTION_IN);
    85	  IOEXP_SETOPTION(ioe, 2, IOEXPANDER_OPTION_INVERT,
    86	                  (void *)IOEXPANDER_VAL_NORMAL);
    87	  IOEXP_SETOPTION(ioe, 2, IOEXPANDER_OPTION_INTCFG,
    88	                  (void *)IOEXPANDER_VAL_BOTH);
    89	  gpio_lower_half(ioe, 2, GPIO_INTERRUPT_PIN, 2);
    90	
    91	  /* Pin 3: a non-inverted, level interrupting pin */
    92
